title: Quantification and Limitations of PSD95-NR2B Clustering using Proximity Ligation Assay in Primary Neuronal Cultures
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fcm2q","text":"Synaptic clustering of proteins plays a crucial role in neuronal functions. These protein interactions are not only technically challenging to quantify but time-consuming even with advanced super-resolution tools. To study the NR2B receptor stabilisation and clustering with PSD95, we employed an optimised high-throughput Proximity Ligation Assay (PLA) protocol for the 96-well optical plate. This is a reproducible protocol with consistent results across replicates. It also enables us to quantify molecular interactions in the primary mouse neuronal cells, with punctate analysed in Opera Phenix using its integrated quantification software. Key improvements in fixing, permeabilisation and nuclear counterstaining for high-throughput compatibility have been made. This method is technically robust, time efficient and provides a reliable tool for synaptic protein interactions. While applied to study NR2B-PSD95 interactions, the quantification of clustering was non-significant throughtout different treatments. These results are from iterated experiments (n=3), where inconclusiveness highlights biological limitations rather than technical. This document includes plausible interpretations of the equivocal data and outlines the use of this protocol to quantify pre and post synaptic connections for plasticity studies. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"980sk","text":"Principle of the experiment: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ar3fp","text":"Proximity ligation assay enables the study of protein-protein interactions with spatial resolution, typically within 40nm distance. This method employs a secondary probe conjugated with unique DNA sequence that ligates to form a circular DNA with its complementary strand within proximity. When two proteins are brought together, the circular DNA is amplified using rolling circle replication, generating multiple copies of the DNA. Fluorescently, labelled oligonucleotides hybridise to the amplified product, producing a signal, visualised as puncta in confocal microscope. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1mmi1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ejiq0","text":"Cell culture guidelines: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"88emk","text":"(Below mentioned are optimised and practiced protocol)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cfd09","text":"Primary neurons were dissociated from E17.5, CD1 embryos.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"575m8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"17ca5","text":"PLATING:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2d6tg","text":"Prepare the NB plus (Cat. A#3582901) supplemented with B27+ (Cat. A#3582801), Pen/strep and Glutamax (Cat. #35050-038).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":119}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2oq9v","text":"1) Coat the 96-well Griener senso plate (Cat. #655892) with 0.2mg/ml of Poly L-ornithine (Cat.  P#3655-100mg) overnight at room temperature or in the incubator. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"97g8g","text":"2) Wash the plates with autoclaved milliQ water twice. Perform the third wash using the media (150µl) that is used for plating. Aspirate the media just before the plating and avoid drying. This helped me achieve primary cell attachment to the glass bottom. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"35hvc","text":"3) Plate 10,000 cells/well using the eppendorf multi pipette hand dispenser. Gently mix the cells before plating for even distribution. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6uomq","text":"4) Leave the plate in the hood with the lid, unhandled for 5min before moving it to the incubator. Immediate handling and placing in the incubator may not help with even surface attachment. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6uk5b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ba3","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"csh23","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2nttg","text":"MEDIA CHANGE:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"70kkh","text":"For experiments conducted on DIV 14, change 50% of the media on DIV 7. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b6hd6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a39n0","text":"Neurons looked consistently unhealthy on DIV 17, after media change on DIV 7 and DIV 14. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bn8vs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ceumm","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"1hs6d","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bcsj7","text":"Limitations to study PSD95-NR2B interactions in primary neuronal cultures: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":75}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1g1o7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dnv3r","text":"1) In vitro primary neuronal cultures lack the synaptic and structural organisations unlike brain slices. The lack of spatial arrangement and synaptic architecture may lead to variability in proximity based assays. Notably, this specific interactions have been successfully studied using PLA in brain tissues cited below. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":3,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c4f1q","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"e7k95","text":"2) The overlap of synaptic zones and dendritic shaft region can result in mixed signals. This introduces variability in data interpretation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cdnav","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fs71p","text":"3) Choosing a protein non-native to the sub organelle, selectively or sporadically localised is imperative. In this experiment, NR2B is ubiquitously present which introduces variability making it difficult to distinguish synaptic versus extra synaptic interactions with PSD95. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bfac6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dvgp0","text":"4)Technically, PLA detects proteins present within the proximity. However, the presence of a PLA signal does not necessarily confirm, whether it is a functional or spatial interaction. This spatial interaction does not reflect biological relevance. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4dmb0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f7trk","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":3,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"2b4jm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3sfr9","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":4,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"dng0e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fhvti","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5k8ta","text":"Conclusion:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dlidu","text":"Proximity Ligation Assay (PLA) is a powerful and sensitive method to study protein-protein interactions at sub cellular level. However, the current study highlights the limitations of assessing PSD95-NR2B in primary neuronal culture. In vitro system lacks synaptic architecture, extra-synaptic presence of NR2B makes it difficult to distinguish synaptic connections and its functional interaction. Furthermore, addition of synaptic markers such as homer or synapsin for normalisation may not fully resolve this issue. In cultured neurons, overlap of neurite network can lead to false positives. It is therefore advisable to confirm PLA findings with additional methods like co-immunoprecipitation, before drawing a definitive conclusion. This protocol is well suited for pre and post-synaptic interaction rather than receptor trafficking studies. Although, PLA is claimed to have a single molecule resolution, it is not suitable for receptor dynamics in primary neuronal culture. Advanced techniques like single molecule localisation microscopy or super-resolution microscopy are better suited to investigate nano domains and receptor organisation at synapses. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6gnnq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ig2d","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fqrlh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"notes","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"vvr9","text":"In the 96-well, fill 200µl of the milliQ or cell culture grade water in the outer wells, and fill 100µl of water in the chimney wells to prevent evaporation of the media. Do not handle the plate frequently except for media change or a quick check. ","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}},"1":{"type":"result","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"30nvl","text":"This PLA protocol was performed following the treatment of DIV14 primary mouse neurons with the exogenous compounds. When target proteins, PSD95-NR2B are within the proximity, a punctate is formed, visualised and quantified. Data were normalised using either, punctate/nuclei or punctate/area ratios. While using the puncta/nuclei, try to eliminate non-neuronal cell types that's devoid of MAP2 stain to ensure the neuronal specificity. ","type":"unstyled"},{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"cuvh8","text":"","type":"unstyled"},{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"77ldb","text":"The normalised results from the independent biological replicates were analysed using One-way ANOVA. There was no statistical significance (p\u003e0.05) across different treatment conditions. Random fields at identical positions were imaged for each treatment and technical replicates. The number of nuclei, punctate and the area of MAP2  compared within the biological replicates had no statistical significance.","type":"unstyled"},{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"17mpt","text":"","type":"unstyled"},{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"43e8a","text":"These ambiguous results were concluded after multiple attempts of different experiments. This suggests a possible biological limitations rather than technical inconsistencies. The potential interpretations are explained below.  ","type":"unstyled"},{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"8359c","text":"","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}},"2":{"type":"citation","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"guid":"B1581F8247134BD2A9B251F99B95A1EC","title":"Enhanced Fyn-tau and NR2B-PSD95 interactions in epileptic foci in experimental models and human epilepsy.","protocol_guid":"","journal":"","date":"2024-09-19T00:00:00Z","created_on":"2026-02-10T22:33:37Z","doi":"https://doi.org/10.1093/braincomms/fcae327","authors":"Putra M, Rao NS, Gardner C, Liu G, Trommater J, Bunney M, Gage M, Bassuk AG, Hefti M, Lee G, Thippeswamy T","can_edit":false}},"3":{"type":"citation","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"guid":"C2222F6BFACE4F90AF61CB9494D59279","title":"Image-Based Profiling of Synaptic Connectivity in Primary Neuronal Cell Culture.","protocol_guid":"","journal":"","date":"2018-06-26T00:00:00Z","created_on":"2026-02-10T22:33:37Z","doi":"https://doi.org/10.3389/fnins.2018.00389","authors":"Verstraelen P, Van Dyck M, Verschuuren M, Kashikar ND, Nuydens R, Timmermans JP, De Vos WH","can_edit":false}},"4":{"type":"citation","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"guid":"1B78CA689F384B3F943EAEC13098F906","title":"Systematic Quantification of Synapses in Primary Neuronal Culture.","protocol_guid":"","journal":"","date":"2020-09-07T00:00:00Z","created_on":"2026-02-10T22:33:37Z","doi":"https://doi.org/10.1016/j.isci.2020.101542","authors":"Verstraelen P, Garcia-Diaz Barriga G, Verschuuren M, Asselbergh B, Nuydens R, Larsen PH, Timmermans JP, De Vos WH","can_edit":false}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"35skh","text":"Make sure to include appropriate negative controls (omission of one primary antibody). Prepare all the buffers including wash A and B in a sterile and filtered water. Kindly make a note of the pre-preparation before each step. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"1i9vm","text":"1) Choosing the right and highly specific primary antibody is crucial.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2b0an","text":"2) Ensure the blocking reagent is stored properly. Prolonged storage at recommended 4°C, may reduce the effectiveness of the solution. Its inefficiency can be visualised by improper staining and many unusually faint PLA puncta. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bu6ms","text":"3) Counterstaining with MAP2 after ligation and polymerisation results in smooth dendritic staining but may lead to the loss of PLA puncta during subsequent washing steps. To minimize this issue, MAP2 staining is performed together with the primary antibody incubation step, although this may slightly compromise the quality of MAP2 labelling. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fbk4f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Thermo scientific #62249","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4qn3j","text":"Neurobasal plus medium - Thermo scientific A#3582901","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6lj52","text":"B27 plus - DUO92008","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"38kst","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"coolv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bt8mv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6c2s9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: PRE-PREP: Prepare 4% Paraformaldehyde [PFA, EMS Cat. #1571]: Add 4ml of 10xPBS + 26ml of milliQH 2 O, then add 10ml of 16% PFA.

Rapid fix the cells in 4% PFA at room temperature (RT) for 15-20 mins. Note: Perform it in the fume hood. I used 1ml pipette to remove the media from each well and promptly added approximate volume of 200 - 250ul of PFA to each well using transfer pipette. Washing is not required before fixing.
Wash 3 times in 1xPBS, 5min/wash at room temperature. Note: First wash is critical and must not allow the PFA to dry in the well. Drying of PFA might cause background issues while imaging.

Add 150µl of permeabilisation buﬀer 1xPBS-0.1% saponin and incubate for 10 mins at room temperature. Note: There are many types of detergent including nonidet, NP-40, Triton X-100, Tween-20, digitonin and various origins of saponin. I used saponin, it removes only cholesterol molecules in the membrane. Since my study focuses on receptor dynamics saponin was used.
Wash 3 times in 1xPBS 5min/wash at room temperature.
PRE-PREP: Add 1ml of 1% of saponin [Stock solution stored at 4°C, Cat. Sigma #84510] to 9ml of 1xPBS [no calcium, magnesium] to a final concentration of 0.1% saponin.

PRE-PREP: Turn on the incubator to 37°C. To prepare a pre-humidity chamber, use a small laboratory tray that fits your 96 well plate. Place the pre-soaked blotting paper and add a few millilitres of water to retain the moisture of the paper. Cover the tray with aluminium foil and pre-warm at 37°C.

Add 60µl of DUOLINK blocking buffer to each well and incubate in the pre-humidity chamber for 60min at 37°C. Note: Test the blocking buffer before running the experiment. Refer attachment for more information.

PRE-PREP: Primary antibodies in DUOLINK antibody diluent Mouse Anti-PSD95 [Cat. MA1-046] - 1:500 Rabbit Anti-NR2B [Cat. 06-600] - 1:2000 Chicken Anti-MAP2 [Cat. PA1-10005] - 1:2000 DUOLINK antibody diluent - up to 40μl
After blocking no washes required.
Add 40μl of primary antibodies, incubate overnight 4°C in the pre-humidity chamber.

PRE-PREP: a) Bring the wash buffers A and B to the room temperature. b) Turn on the incubator to 37°C. c) Pre-warm the humidity chamber to 37°C and bring the plate to room temperature. d) Secondary antibodies in DUOLINK antibody diluent Donkey anti-rabbit PLA (+) probe [Cat. DUO92002] - 1:5 Donkey anti-mouse PLA (-) probe [Cat. DUO92004] - 1:5 Goat anti-chicken Alexa 488 [Cat. ab150173] - 1:1000 DUOLINK antibody diluent - up to 40μl
Wash the wells 2 times in buffer A for 5min/wash at RT. Note: During washes leave the pre-humidity chamber in the incubator.
Add 40µl of secondary antibodies, incubate in the pre-humidity chamber for 1hr at 37°C.
PRE-PREP: Prepare the ligation mix based on the number of samples. For a 40μl reaction, High purity or PCR grade water - 31μl 5x Ligation buffer [1:5] - 8μl Ligase enzyme [1:40] - 1μl
Wash the wells 2 times in buffer A for 5min/wash at RT. Note: During washes leave the pre-humidity chamber in the incubator.
Add 40μl of ligation mixture, incubate in the pre-humidity chamber at 37°C for 30mins.
PRE-PREP: Light sensitive reagents DUOLINK polymerisation assay for 40ul reaction, High purity or PCR grade water - 31.5μl 5x Polymerisation buffer [1:5] - 8μl Polymerase enzyme [1:80] - 0.5μl
Wash the wells 2 times in buffer A for 5min/wash at RT. Note: During washes leave the pre-humidity chamber in the incubator.
Add 40μl of amplification mixture, incubate in the pre-humidity chamber at 37°C for 100mins.
PRE-PREP: Prepare required volume of HOECHST (1μg/ml, Cat. Thermo scientific #62249) in wash buffer B.
Wash once in buffer B for 10min at RT. Note: Pre-humidity chamber is not required from this step.
Incubate with HOECHST for 15min at RT.
Wash 2 times in buffer B for 10min at RT.
Wash in 0.01x buffer B for 1min at RT.
Add 150μl of 1xPBS and perform the imaging. Imaged using Opera Phenix High Content Screening equipped with 40x water immersion objective (Numerical Aperture 1.1). Pixel size = 0.32µm per pixel.
creator: Suba Soundarya Santhi Anandhan
